subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: governance/rights/politics rebuilt from independent sources (IMF Art IV 2025, World Bank, Freedom House, RSF, HRW, TI, Carnegie); Kuwaiti state media EXCLUDED there, with economic figures admitted only via IMF/rating-agency validation.

Governance regression: the emir suspended parliament in 2024 under a multi-year constitutional review. Migrant-majority reality: citizens are a ~30% minority and a large stateless 'Bidoon' population faces abuse.
Standout features: one of the world's largest sovereign funds and very low crime, against near-total oil dependence, a 2024 governance regression, and extreme heat/water scarcity.

Recalibrated 2026-05-29 for the 2026 Iran war (Operation Epic Fury, US+Israel vs Iran from 28 Feb) and the Strait of Hormuz closure: blockade of ~1/5 of world oil+LNG, attacks/threats on Gulf desalination (water security), and the global energy shock. The two-week 8 Apr ceasefire collapsed by late May — the US renewed strikes on Iran ~25-26 May (Trump convened Camp David) and the war resumed, with Hormuz still blockaded. (Reuters; BBC; NYT; CNN; ISW; Al Jazeera; CSIS; Wikipedia)

economic.notes: A vast sovereign-wealth buffer and sound banks, against near-total oil dependence and a basket-peg loss of monetary autonomy.

fiscal_state.notes: Huge reserves cushion solvency, but the Hormuz shutdown halts the oil revenue the budget runs on. (IMF)

monetary_independence.notes: An undisclosed-basket peg means essentially no autonomous policy, but durable fund-backed stability - a mild-negative trade-off. (IMF; CBK)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: Not a reserve currency, but peg stability and the sovereign fund's global footprint warrant a notch up. (IMF)

inflation.notes: Low and easing - a peg strength. (IMF 2025 Art IV)

banking_stability.notes: Sound, well-capitalised, and sovereign-backed (high investment-grade ratings). (Moody's; S&P)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: Almost all oil exports route through the blockaded Strait of Hormuz with no bypass — a near-total export-revenue stop. (Reuters; BBC)

institutional.notes: Oil-funded delivery, against a 2024 power concentration and a declining press.

rule_of_law.notes: A quasi-absolute monarchy; the emir suspended parliament and key constitutional articles in 2024, concentrating power. (Carnegie; TI)

statistical_integrity.notes: Economic data is IMF-validated; governance data is opaque. (IMF)

civil_service_capacity.notes: Oil-funded and functional but bloated and citizen-employment-driven, slowing delivery. (World Bank; IMF)

press_freedom.notes: Once the Gulf's freest, now declining, with national-security prosecutions of critics post-suspension. (RSF; Freedom House 2025)

political_social.notes: Wealthy and orderly, against a governance regression, restricted liberties, and a citizen-minority structure.

political_stability.notes: Wealthy and orderly, but the 2024 parliamentary suspension plus decades of executive-legislative gridlock signal a governance regression. (Carnegie)

civil_liberties.notes: Restricted and tightening post-2024, with speech prosecutions. (Freedom House 2025; HRW)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: Easy employer-sponsored residency and no income tax, but citizens are a ~30% minority under kafala-legacy controls with no citizenship path, plus a large stateless 'Bidoon' population. (HRW 2025)

social_cohesion.notes: Orderly and low-crime, but with a structural citizen/non-citizen cleavage. (comparative)

demographics.notes: A small young citizen base atop a migrant-majority workforce. (World Bank)

geopolitical.notes: A close US ally with an open account, against Gulf/Hormuz exposure.

alliance_reliability.notes: A close US ally (a major non-NATO ally) and GCC-backed. (US State Dept)

conflict_involvement.notes: Struck by Iranian fire and on the front line of the 2026 Gulf war. (Al Jazeera)

physical_practical.notes: Good healthcare and very low crime, against extreme heat and water scarcity.

climate.notes: Among the hottest inhabited places - near-unliveable wet-bulb heat by mid-century, dust, desal-dependent water, with extreme wealth/AC offset. (IPCC AR6; WB)

infrastructure.notes: Wholly desalination-dependent for water; Kuwait's electricity-and-water ministry publicly raised the alarm after Iran's threats to Gulf water plants. (Al Jazeera; CSIS)

crime_safety.notes: Very low crime - a clear strength. (comparative)

disaster_insurance.notes: Low natural-catastrophe exposure aside from heat and water stress. (hazard context)

summary: A wealthy, low-crime, US-allied oil state with one of the world's largest sovereign funds (driving a large general-government surplus despite a flow deficit) and sound, highly-rated banks. The offsets are near-total oil dependence and the Gulf's most gridlocked policymaking, a 2024 governance regression in which the emir suspended parliament, a declining press, a citizen-minority workforce with no path to belonging (and a stateless 'Bidoon' population), and an extreme heat and water-scarcity tail. A basket peg trades monetary autonomy for stability.
